ingredients.item:
Canh riêu is a classic Vietnamese soup made from pounded seafood (rice paddy crab, shrimp, or fish) and a stock base of tomato. In this version, you'll use tofu crumbles and minced mushrooms as your protein substitutions. The cilantro and annatto oil create a similar flavor and color to the original dish.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD4 servings
5½ ounces (150 g) firm tofu, finely crumbled with a fork
5½ ounces (150 g) silken tofu, finely crumbled with a fork
2 teaspoons vegetable or mushroom stock powder, divided
½ teaspoon salt, plus more if needed
½ teaspoon sugar
ingredients.original:
Canh riêu is a classic Vietnamese soup made from pounded seafood (rice paddy crab, shrimp, or fish) and a stock base of tomato. In this version, you'll use tofu crumbles and minced mushrooms as your protein substitutions. The cilantro and annatto oil create a similar flavor and color to the original dish.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD4 servings
5½ ounces (150 g) firm tofu, finely crumbled with a fork
5½ ounces (150 g) silken tofu, finely crumbled with a fork
2 teaspoons vegetable or mushroom stock powder, divided
½ teaspoon salt, plus more if needed
½ teaspoon sugar

ingredients.item: 1 cube store-bought or homemade chao, finely crushed
ingredients.original: 1 cube store-bought or homemade chao, finely crushed

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) minced enoki mushrooms, stems trimmed and rinsed with salted water
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) minced enoki mushrooms, stems trimmed and rinsed with salted water

ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) sawtooth herb or cilantro, finely chopped, for garnish
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) sawtooth herb or cilantro, finely chopped, for garnish

ingredients.item: 1. In a large bowl, combine both types of tofu, 1 teaspoon of the stock powder, salt, sugar, chao, fermented soybean sauce, annatto oil, and pepper, and mix well. Cover and let sit for 15 minutes to allow the flavor to develop.
ingredients.original: 1. In a large bowl, combine both types of tofu, 1 teaspoon of the stock powder, salt, sugar, chao, fermented soybean sauce, annatto oil, and pepper, and mix well. Cover and let sit for 15 minutes to allow the flavor to develop.

ingredients.item: 2. In a medium skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of the vegetable oil over low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until fragrant (about 1 minute). Add the tofu mixture and flatten its surface with a spatula. Cook on low until the mixture is set (2 to 3 more minutes), and then remove from heat.
ingredients.original: 2. In a medium skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of the vegetable oil over low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until fragrant (about 1 minute). Add the tofu mixture and flatten its surface with a spatula. Cook on low until the mixture is set (2 to 3 more minutes), and then remove from heat.

ingredients.item: 3. In a medium saucepan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon vegetable oil over medium heat. Add tomatoes and cook and stir until soft (about 3 minutes). Add water and bring to a boil.
ingredients.original: 3. In a medium saucepan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon vegetable oil over medium heat. Add tomatoes and cook and stir until soft (about 3 minutes). Add water and bring to a boil.

ingredients.item: 4. Add mushrooms and the remaining 1 teaspoon stock powder, taste, and add more salt if needed. Cook over medium heat until tomatoes are broken down and mushrooms are soft (about 2 more minutes), and then remove from heat.
ingredients.original: 4. Add mushrooms and the remaining 1 teaspoon stock powder, taste, and add more salt if needed. Cook over medium heat until tomatoes are broken down and mushrooms are soft (about 2 more minutes), and then remove from heat.

ingredients.item: 5. Transfer to a large serving bowl or divide into smaller serving bowls.
ingredients.original: 5. Transfer to a large serving bowl or divide into smaller serving bowls.

ingredients.item: 6. Add the tofu mixture (riêu) to the hot soup, garnish with sawtooth herb or cilantro, and serve hot.
ingredients.original: 6. Add the tofu mixture (riêu) to the hot soup, garnish with sawtooth herb or cilantro, and serve hot.
